pass_context.methodology: RF-13 is a META pass — analyzes the SHAPE of exchanges (delta scoring, cycle detection, trajectory analysis) rather than individual message content. Uses RF-13-SUPPLEMENT.md Section 1 (delta scale), Section 2 (escalation shapes), Section 3 (cycle detection), Section 4 (temporal correlation).

triage_profile.note: 'Run LAST Tier 1 — needs full cross-RF picture.' Full cross-RF picture now available (8 prior passes complete).

cross_rf_foundation.rf04_arc: ARC-RF04-001 (89 signals, full dataset span) provides the primary escalation episode map. RF-04 flags are concentrated in received direction — Melissa is the dominant initiator/escalator across the dataset.
cross_rf_foundation.rf05_arc: ARC-RF05-001 (26 signals) documents legal threats as peak-escalation mechanisms — threats are the ceiling of the escalation scale in this dataset.
cross_rf_foundation.rf07_arc: ARC-RF07-001 (12 signals) documents flooding patterns — rapid-sequence messages are temporal escalation indicators.
cross_rf_foundation.direction_imbalance: Triage: 86 received / 39 sent RF-13 keyword hits (69% received). Combined with RF-04 directionality (93 flags majority received), consistent-escalator pattern is pre-confirmed.

temporal_patterns_observed: Holiday cluster: Nov 2022 (3 hostile eps in 3 days); Dec 2022, Dec 2023 (Christmas hostile eps)
February pattern: Feb 14 2023 (104 msgs), Feb 9 2025 (68 msgs), Feb 15 2025 (164 msgs) — repeated Feb escalation
June 2024 sustained cluster: 4 hostile episodes over 3 weeks (EP-0793, EP-0808, EP-0809, EP-0810)
Aug 2024 mega-period: EP-0854 (311 msgs Aug 4) + EP-0861, EP-0871, EP-0873 all hostile same month
Jan-Feb 2025 cluster: EP-0996 (106 msgs Jan 15) + EP-1018 (164 msgs Feb 15)
Phase C sparse (4 hostile eps) despite being the most legally consequential period — structural shift from direct-exchange conflict to visit-refusal/legal-proceedings conflict

anticipated_arcs: ARC-RF13-001: Escalation Trajectory (expected ACTIVE — clearly worsening Phase A→C)
ARC-RF13-002: Role Consistency (Melissa = consistent initiator/escalator; Eli = de-escalator)
ARC-RF13-003: De-Escalation Resistance (Melissa consistently ignores Eli's de-escalation attempts)
anticipated_cycles: CYC-001: Abby-trigger cycle — any Abby reference triggers Melissa escalation
CYC-002: Schedule/logistics spike — minor logistical request → rapid hostility
CYC-003: Holiday escalation — Dec/Feb periods reliably hostile
CYC-004: Legal-threat ceiling — disputes reach peak → legal threat deployed as escalation ceiling
CYC-005: Child-welfare allegation — conflict climax → harm allegation deployment

calibration_episode.description: Aug 28 2022 church etiquette dispute (ROW-50-61). Documented in RF-04 as the B-001 DARVO signal and in RF-09 as peripheral. Eli raises concern about Olivia's behavior at church (disrespectful); Melissa pivots to attacking Eli's own church behavior ('I've seen you draw in your notebook'). This is the baseline escalation event in the dataset.
calibration_episode.reason_not_flagged: Net escalation 1.5 is below Sev-1 threshold (>3.0). Single tense episode in an otherwise cooperative baseline period. Establishes the fundamental asymmetric dynamic (Eli attempts mild de-escalation; Melissa escalates with DARVO pivot) but one instance does not constitute a primary RF-13 flag. Noted as CANDIDATE arc signal only.

batch_summary.note: Jan–Oct 2022 calibration batch. Zero RF-13 primary flags. This is the cooperative baseline period — 32 episodes, predominantly neutral. EP-0009 (Aug 28, 32 msgs, tense) is the only non-neutral episode and establishes the baseline asymmetric dynamic: Melissa escalates with DARVO pivot when Eli raises a concern, but the net escalation (1.5) is below primary threshold. The asymmetric pattern (Eli de-escalates, Melissa does not) is already visible in the first conflict episode but not flaggable at one instance. Arc ARC-RF13-002 (role consistency) initialized at CANDIDATE: baseline data point 1/N collected.

arc_updates.event: Trajectory arc initialized — Phase A cooperative baseline established
Role consistency arc initialized — EP-0009 establishes baseline: Melissa = escalator, Eli = de-escalator in the first tense episode
De-escalation resistance arc initialized — EP-0009: Eli de-escalation attempt not acknowledged by Melissa
